steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: Ortega argues that a society's spiritual well-being depends on the character of its literary culture. The supporting quote must directly assert that literature is vital to a people's inner life or spirit.

steps.title: Match a quote that ties literature to spiritual vitality
steps.content: Choice A — "Poetry, which brings together or separates, which fortifies or brings anguish, which shores up or demolishes souls, which gives or robs men of faith and vigor, is more necessary to a people than industry itself, for industry provides them with a means of subsistence, while literature gives them the desire and strength for life." says poetry "fortifies or brings anguish," "shores up or demolishes souls," and "gives them the desire and strength for life" — literature is cast as more necessary than industry because it sustains the spirit. That is exactly the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "Poetry, which brings together or separates, which fortifies or brings anguish, which shores up or demolishes souls, which gives or robs men of faith and vigor, is more necessary to a people than industry itself, for industry provides them with a means of subsistence, while literature gives them the desire and strength for life.": directly links literature to a society's spiritual strength and well-being. ✓
  • Choice B — "Every society brings to literature its own form of expression, and the history of the nations can be told with greater truth by the stages of literature than by chronicles and decades.": makes literature a historical record, not a source of spiritual well-being — misses the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — "Where will a race of men go when they have lost the habit of thinking with faith about the scope and meaning of their actions? The best among them, those who consecrate Nature with their sacred desire for the future, will lose, in a sordid and painful annihilation, all stimulus to alleviate the ugliness of humanity.": laments loss of faith and thinking but does not say a society's spirit depends on the character of its literary culture. ✗
  • Choice D — "Listen to the song of this hardworking and satisfied nation; listen to Walt Whitman. The exercise of himself exalts him to majesty, tolerance exalts him to justice, and order to joy.": praises one poet's personal virtues; does not state that society's spiritual well-being depends on its literature. ✗
The poetry quotation is the correct support.
